# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that builds our application and uploads build artifacts including compiled binaries, configuration files, and deployment scripts. Make the artifacts available for team members to download. This is for our internal build distribution system.

Prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Build Artifact Generation Workflow
on: [push]  # or manually trigger with API (e.g., HTTP POST)
jobs:  
# This job will be triggered on every push to the repository and pull requests, but only for the 'main' branch changes are considered here as we want our build artifacts available in a specific folder within GitHub repo 
  build-artifacts:    # Job name is used by workflow. You can use any valid string or number (alphanumeric), it will be unique per job and step, but cannot contain special characters like / : @ * ? " < >  
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest     # Run on the latest version of Ubuntu 20.04 LTS server to ensure compatibility with our codebase changes frequently (e.g., updates)     
    steps:      
        - name: Checkout Repository          # This action will check out your repository so you can access its contents  
            uses: actions/checkout@v2         # Use the official GitHub Actions 'actions' namespace to perform a set of common tasks in workflows.  The syntax is `namespace/action-name`, but not all are available on every namespace or action    
        - name: Setup NodeJS Environment      # This step will install any dependencies your project needs (specified as part of package.json)  
          uses: actions/setup-node@v2       # Use the official GitHub Actions 'actions' namespace to perform a set up node environment action    
          with: 
            node-version: "14"               # Specify version you want to use, e.g., v14 (latest), stable or any other version  
        - name: Install Dependencies           # This step will install all dependencies your project needs using npm and yarn   
          run: |                             # Run a single command with the `run` keyword    
            npm ci                            # Use 'npm ci' to ensure that you have installed package.json’s devDependencies as well  
        - name: Build Application               # This step will build your application using webpack or another tool of choice   
          run: |                               # Run a single command with the `run` keyword    
            npm run build                       # Use 'npm' to execute our custom script that builds out app (specified in package.json)  
        - name: Archive Artifacts               # This step will archive your artifacts, ie., files you want shared or uploaded with the workflow   
          uses: actions/upload-artifact@v2     # Use 'actions' namespace to perform an action that uploads a file (like creating releases)  
          with: 
            name: "Build Artifacts"             # Name of artifact, can be any string. It will appear as the title in workflow run details   
            path: |                               # Path is where your build output should go on disk    
              ./build                           # Specify a directory to upload (relative or absolute paths are allowed)
